title: per-host runtimes via the bringup kit
manager: Adding host N stops being a research project — port a roughly 200-line harness, run the conformance corpus, declare your tier. Python and C follow the v6 Rust runtime; demand picks the rest. The bringup kit is what turns "support another language" from a months-long port into a bounded, checklist-driven task, because each runtime is just an IR consumer plus a C2 stepper measured against the shared vectors.
cs: bringup kit = corpus runner, trace comparator, canonical-value serialization tests, IR loader tests, ABI mocks, negative diagnostics, replay tests, manifest validator; each runtime = IR consumer + C2 stepper (T1 first); tier growth by passing more vectors, never by assertion.
